contentBlocks.original:
ॐ अग्निमीळे पुरोहितं यज्ञस्य देवमृत्विजम् ।
होतारं रत्नधातमम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ŏm āgnim-īille Purohitam ẏajnyasya ḍevam-ṟtvijam |
ḥotāram ṟatna-ḍhātamam ||1||
contentBlocks.translation: I glorify Agni, the high priest of sacrifice, the divine, the ministrant, who is the offerer and possessor of greatest wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (om) – sacred sound; अग्निम् (agnim) – Agni; ईळे (īḷe) – I glorify; पुरोहितम् (purohitam) – high priest; यज्ञस्य (yajñasya) – of sacrifice; देवम् (devam) – divine; ऋत्विजम् (ṛtvijam) – ministrant; होतारम् (hotāram) – offerer; रत्नधातमम् (ratnadhātamam) – possessor of greatest wealth (ratna:wealth | dhātamam:greatest possessor);

contentBlocks.original:
अग्निः पूर्वेभिर्ऋषिभिरीड्यो नूतनैरुत ।
स देवाँ एह वक्षति ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agniḥ pūrvebhirṛṣibhirīḍyo nūtanairuta .
sa devām̐ eha vakṣati ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: May that Agni, who is worthy to be praised by ancient and modern sages, gather the Gods here.
contentBlocks.meanings: अग्निः (agniḥ) – Agni; पूर्वेभिः (pūrvebhiḥ) – by the ancients; ऋषिभिः (ṛṣibhiḥ) – by sages; ईड्यः (īḍyaḥ) – worthy to be praised; नूतनैः (nūtanaiḥ) – by the moderns; उत (uta) – and; सः (saḥ) – he; देवान् (devān) – the Gods; एह (eha) – here; वक्षति (vakṣati) – will gather;

contentBlocks.translation: Through Agni, one gets lot of wealth that increases day by day. One gets fame and the best progeny.
contentBlocks.meanings: अग्निना (agninā) – through Agni; रयिम् (rayim) – wealth; अश्नवत् (aśnavat) – one gets; पोषम् (poṣam) – nourishment; एव (eva) – indeed; दिवे (dive) – day; दिवे (dive) – by day; यशसं (yaśasaṃ) – fame; वीरवत्तमम् (vīravattamam) – best progeny (vīra:hero | vattama:best);

contentBlocks.original:
अग्ने यं यज्ञमध्वरं विश्वतः परिभूरसि ।
स इद्देवेषु गच्छति ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agne yaṃ yajñamadhvaraṃ viśvataḥ paribhūrasi .
sa iddeveṣu gacchati ..4..
contentBlocks.translation: O Agni, you are surrounding the non-violent sacrifice on all sides, that which indeed reaches (in) the gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: अग्ने (agne) – O Agni; यं (yaṃ) – which; यज्ञम् (yajñam) – sacrifice; अध्वरम् (adhvaram) – non-violent; विश्वतः (viśvataḥ) – on all sides; परिभूः (paribhūḥ) – surrounding; असि (asi) – you are; सः (saḥ) – that; इत् (it) – indeed; देवेषु (deveṣu) – in the gods; गच्छति (gacchati) – reaches;

contentBlocks.original:
अग्निर्होता कविक्रतुः सत्यश्चित्रश्रवस्तमः ।
देवो देवेभिरा गमत् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agnirhotā kavikratuḥ satyaścitraśravastamaḥ .
devo devebhirā gamat ..5..
contentBlocks.translation: May Agni, the sacrificer, one who possesses immense wisdom, he who is true, has most distinguished fame, is divine, come hither with the gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: अग्निः (agniḥ) – Agni; होता (hotā) – sacrificer; कविक्रतुः (kavikratuḥ) – possessor of immense wisdom (kavi:wise | kratuḥ:intellect); सत्यः (satyaḥ) – true; चित्रश्रवस्तमः (citraśravastamaḥ) – most distinguished fame (citra:distinguished | śravas:fame | tamaḥ:most); देवः (devaḥ) – divine; देवेभिः (devebhiḥ) – with the gods; आ (ā) – come; गमत् (gamat) – hither;

contentBlocks.original:
यदङ्ग दाशुषे त्वमग्ने भद्रं करिष्यसि ।
तवेत्तत्सत्यमङ्गिरः ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadaṅga dāśuṣe tvamagne bhadraṃ kariṣyasi .
tavettatsatyamaṅgiraḥ ..6..
contentBlocks.translation: O Agni, whatever good you will do and whatever possessions you bestow (upon the worshipper), that, O Angiras, is indeed your essence.
contentBlocks.meanings: यदङ्ग (yadaṅga) – whatever; दाशुषे (dāśuṣe) – to the worshipper; त्वम् (tvam) – you; अग्ने (agne) – O Agni; भद्रं (bhadraṃ) – good; करिष्यसि (kariṣyasi) – you will do; तव (tava) – your; एतत् (etat) – this; सत्यं (satyaṃ) – truth; अङ्गिरः (aṅgiraḥ) – O Angiras;

contentBlocks.original:
उप त्वाग्ने दिवेदिवे दोषावस्तर्धिया वयम् ।
नमो भरन्त एमसि ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
upa tvāgne divedive doṣāvastardhiyā vayam .
namo bharanta emasi ..7..
contentBlocks.translation: O Agni, the illuminer (dispeller) of darkness, we approach near thee (thy vicinity) with thought (willingness), day by day, while bearing obeisance.
contentBlocks.meanings: उप (upa) – near; त्वा (tvā) – thee; अग्ने (agne) – O Agni; दिवे (dive) – day; दिवे (dive) – day; दोषा (doṣā) – darkness; अवः (avaḥ) – dispeller; तर्धिया (tardhiyā) – with thought; वयम् (vayam) – we; नमः (namaḥ) – obeisance; भरन्त (bharanta) – bearing; एमसि (emasi) – approach;

contentBlocks.original:
राजन्तमध्वराणां गोपामृतस्य दीदिविम् ।
वर्धमानं स्वे दमे ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rājantamadhvarāṇāṃ gopāmṛtasya dīdivim .
vardhamānaṃ sve dame ..8..
contentBlocks.translation: We approach thee, the shining (the radiant), the protector of noninjuring sacrifices, growing in your own dwelling, the bright star of truth.
contentBlocks.meanings: राजन्तम् (rājantam) – the shining; अध्वराणाम् (adhvarāṇām) – of sacrifices; गोपाम् (gopām) – protector; अमृतस्य (amṛtasya) – of immortality; दीदिविम् (dīdivim) – the radiant; वर्धमानम् (vardhamānam) – growing; स्वे (sve) – in own; दमे (dame) – dwelling;

contentBlocks.original:
स नः पितेव सूनवेऽग्ने सूपायनो भव ।
सचस्वा नः स्वस्तये ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa naḥ piteva sūnave'gne sūpāyano bhava .
sacasvā naḥ svastaye ..9..
contentBlocks.translation: O Agni, be easily accesible to us, like a father to his son. Accompany us for our well being.
contentBlocks.meanings: स (sa) – he; नः (naḥ) – to us; पिता (pitā) – father; इव (iva) – like; सूनवे (sūnave) – to son; अग्ने (agne) – O Agni; सुपायनः (supāyanaḥ) – easily accessible (su:good | pāyanaḥ:accessible); भव (bhava) – be; सचस्व (sacasva) – accompany; नः (naḥ) – us; स्वस्तये (svastaye) – for well being;
